programs.name: Basketball
programs.description: Hit the court for non-stop basketball play. Whether you are a new or experienced player, want to play often or occasionally, drop-in basketball offers something for everyone. Though not formally structured, guidelines are used to ensure participation is balanced. Supervision, use of equipment and capacity limits vary by location, please check ahead.It is free to reserve a spot in this activity, fees may apply at the time of check in. Please arrive 10 minutes before your scheduled start time to check in with staff and pay. If you do not arrive within 5 minutes of your start time, your spot will be given to someone in the walk-in line. A printed receipt or a mobile screenshot is required at check in.Participants are permitted to bring their own personal equipment.To cancel, visit your online account, speak with community centre staff, or contact Client Services at 416-396-7378 during regular business hours.

programs.description: Be part of a team! Our basketball house league activity is structured to first teach you the basic skills and rules of the game, and then put them into action through recreational level competitive play. programs.name: CampTO Plus Museums: Secret Garden at Spadina Museum
programs.description: Be immersed in the beautiful gardens to learn about nature, plants, wildlife and the history of this land. Using seasonal produce of the kitchen garden, make delicious treats in the kitchen of the historic coach house. Inspired by nature and the beautiful art in the museum, this camp has plenty of arts and crafts for children to unleash their imagination and creativity to express themselves through paint, clay, collage and more.  Campers bring their curiosity and travel back in time to the 1920s to discover the secrets of the house and uncover amazing and surprising history.  In addition to the extensive outdoor space, campers have a cool and comfortable home base in the and make special visits throughout the historic mansion as well.

programs.name: CampTO Plus Museums: Todmorden Mills
programs.description: CampTO at Todmorden Mills (10-12) Participate in crafts and activities that encourage movement, theatre elements and in-depth explorations into history and the natural world. Experience the unique history and natural landscape of Todmorden Mills through special guided tours of the Wildflower Preserve, Papermill Gallery and two historic homes.

programs.name: CampTO Plus: Arts and Nature
programs.description: This camp combines the study of ecology and art. Campers will have an opportunity to learn about preservation and the identification of plants and animals by exploring the environment. As well, using nature as inspiration, campers will create art projects, from drawings and paintings to relief art and sculpture.In addition to the minimum two hours a day focusing on arts and nature, you will also enjoy games, arts, sports and swim days dependent on weather and camp location. A trip or on-site special experience will be included, such as themed events, clinics or workshops to further enhance the camp program.

programs.name: CampTO Plus: Basketball
programs.description: Calling all basketball fans! Through practice and play, you will build on skills like dribbling, passing, layups and correct ball handling while demonstrating respectful play.In addition to the minimum two hours a day focusing on developing and fine-tuning your skills in basketball, you will also enjoy games, arts, sports and swim days dependent on weather and camp location. A trip or on-site special experience will be included, such as themed events, clinics or workshops to further enhance the camp.

programs.name: Aquatic Fitness Conference and Instructor Certification
programs.description: The City of Toronto and WaterArt International are cohosting a unique opportunity for anyone interested in aquatic fitness. The conference offers a great opportunity to be introduced to new techniques and learn about emerging trends in the aquatic fitness industry. Enjoy a variety of workshop sessions in both shallow and deep water, as well as theory workshops.The Shallow/Deep Aquatic Fitness Instructor Certification provides the opportunity to complete the theoretical portion of becoming a certified Aquatic Fitness Instructor. Skills will be taught through a combination of classroom and in-pool sessions. Practical and written examinations will be components of this certification.For more information on this activity, visit: https://www.toronto.ca/explore-enjoy/parks-recreation/program-activities/leadership-employment-readiness/aquatic-fitness-certifications/For employment with the City of Toronto, Standard First Aid CPR-C is required, visit www.toronto.ca/recskills.

programs.name: CampTO Plus: Island Explorer
programs.description: Climb on board our ferries to be transported to the City's oasis, Toronto Island, for this complete island experience.  Here you will visit the island lagoons, the Franklin Children’s Garden, and the Gibraltar Lighthouse where you will learn and practice canoeing, fishing and urban gardening. As you explore, you will learn the history of the island and its significance as a place of relaxation to a growing City of Toronto in the early 20th century. The ferry fee is included in the cost of the camp.

programs.name: CampTO Plus Museums: Secret Garden at Spadina Museum
programs.description: Be immersed in the beautiful gardens to learn about nature, plants, wildlife and the history of this land. Using seasonal produce of the kitchen garden, make delicious treats in the kitchen of the historic coach house. Inspired by nature and the beautiful art in the museum, this camp has plenty of arts and crafts for children to unleash their imagination and creativity to express themselves through paint, clay, collage and more.  Campers bring their curiosity and travel back in time to the 1920s to discover the secrets of the house and uncover amazing and surprising history.  In addition to the extensive outdoor space, campers have a cool and comfortable home base in the and make special visits throughout the historic mansion as well.

programs.name: CampTO Plus Museums: Todmorden Mills
programs.description: CampTO at Todmorden Mills (10-12) Participate in crafts and activities that encourage movement, theatre elements and in-depth explorations into history and the natural world. Experience the unique history and natural landscape of Todmorden Mills through special guided tours of the Wildflower Preserve, Papermill Gallery and two historic homes.

programs.name: CampTO Plus: Arts and Nature
programs.description: This camp combines the study of ecology and art. Campers will have an opportunity to learn about preservation and the identification of plants and animals by exploring the environment. As well, using nature as inspiration, campers will create art projects, from drawings and paintings to relief art and sculpture.In addition to the minimum two hours a day focusing on arts and nature, you will also enjoy games, arts, sports and swim days dependent on weather and camp location. A trip or on-site special experience will be included, such as themed events, clinics or workshops to further enhance the camp program.

programs.name: Aquatic Fitness Instructor Certification
programs.description: The Aquatic Fitness Instructor Certification course is offered by WaterART Fitness International. This certification provides instructors with the  skills they require to teach an engaging and safe aquatic fitness class in both shallow and deep water. These skills include class management, water movements, understanding major muscle groups, joint action, and how to design target training sets. Skills will be taught through a combination of classroom and in-pool sessions. Practical and written examinations will also be components of this certification.For more information on this activity, visit https://www.toronto.ca/explore-enjoy/parks-recreation/program-activities/leadership-employment-readiness/aquatic-fitness-certifications/For employment with the City of Toronto, Standard Frist Aid CPR-C is required, visit www.toronto.ca/recskills.

programs.name: CampTO Plus: Arts and Nature
programs.description: This camp combines the study of ecology and art. Campers will have an opportunity to learn about preservation and the identification of plants and animals by exploring the environment. As well, using nature as inspiration, campers will create art projects, from drawings and paintings to relief art and sculpture. In addition to the minimum two hours a day focusing on arts and nature, you will also enjoy games, arts, sports and swim days dependent on weather and camp location. A trip or on-site special experience will be included, such as themed events, clinics or workshops to further enhance the camp program.

programs.name: CampTO Plus: Island Explorer
programs.description: Climb on board our ferries to be transported to the City's oasis, Toronto Island, for this complete island experience.  Here you will visit the island lagoons, the Franklin Children’s Garden, and the Gibraltar Lighthouse where you will learn and practice canoeing, fishing and urban gardening. As you explore, you will learn the history of the island and its significance as a place of relaxation to a growing City of Toronto in the early 20th century. The ferry fee is included in the cost of the camp.

A trip or on-site special experience will be included, such as themed events, clinics, or workshops to further enhance the camp program.

programs.name: A Journey from Acrylics to Oils
programs.description: This introductory–intermediate course is for beginners and experienced painters who want to explore oil painting using water-mixable oils. Often seen as intimidating or toxic, oil painting becomes safe and accessible in this class. Students will learn layering, glazing, and composition while building confidence and personal expression.

programs.name: Abstract & Surreal Landscapes
programs.description: Move  beyond traditional depictions of nature and explore more abstract, expressive  approaches to landscape. Through painting, drawing, and sketching, discover how  colour, shape and composition can capture the spirit and emotion of a place  rather than its literal form. Experiment with layering, mark-making and dynamic  brushwork to translate the emotional and atmospheric qualities of the natural  world into an abstract visual language.

programs.name: Abstract Acrylic Painting: Composition
programs.description: Express yourself with paint with a focus on abstract composition. Experiment with novel ways of applying acrylic paint on canvas, such as squeegee paint, drip and splatter, flow painting and mixed media collage. Delve into the history of abstract painting, and the elements and principles of composition. Beginners and Intermediate welcome. Materials list will be provided.

programs.name: Swim and Lifesaving Instructor
programs.description: This course is offered in collaboration with the Lifesaving Society. Participants learn to organize, plan, teach and evaluate swimming, lifesaving and first aid skills. Discover and develop skills in the areas of class management, teaching, evaluation, leadership, communication and administration. With the successful completion of this course, candidates will be qualified to instruct the Toronto Learn to Swim program, the Lifesaving Society Swim for Life program, Emergency First Aid with CPR-B, Rookie, Ranger and Star Patrol as well as Bronze Star, Medallion and Cross.Prerequisite: Minimum age of 15 years old and Bronze Cross (current or expired).For information about attendance, withdrawals and refunds for courses offered with the Lifesaving Society, visit www.toronto.ca/recskills.

programs.name: Swim and Lifesaving Instructor
programs.description: This course is offered in collaboration with the Lifesaving Society. Participants learn to organize, plan, teach and evaluate swimming, lifesaving and first aid skills. Discover and develop skills in the areas of class management, teaching, evaluation, leadership, communication and administration. With the successful completion of this course, candidates will be qualified to instruct the Toronto Learn to Swim program, the Lifesaving Society Swim for Life program, Emergency First Aid with CPR-B, Rookie, Ranger and Star Patrol as well as Bronze Star, Medallion and Cross.Prerequisite: Minimum age of 15 years old and Bronze Cross (current or expired).For information about attendance, withdrawals and refunds for courses offered with the Lifesaving Society, visit www.toronto.ca/recskills.

programs.description: Calling all sports fans! In this camp you will have the opportunity to learn the fundamentals of play in a variety of sports. programs.name: Emergency First Aid (CPR B)
programs.description: This course is offered in collaboration with the Lifesaving Society. Candidates will learn first-aid principles and the emergency treatment of injuries. This course teaches victim assessment, CPR, choking and how to respond to external bleeding, heart attack, stroke, wounds and burns.For information about attendance, withdrawals and refunds for courses offered by the Lifesaving Society, visit www.toronto.ca/recskills.

programs.name: Examiner Course
programs.description: This course is offered in collaboration with the Lifesaving Society. The Examiner Course is the first step in a three-step process to become certified as an Examiner for the Lifesaving Society. The Examiner Course prepares candidates to apprentice successfully as an Examiner on the exam of their choice, and it builds on the evaluation experience that candidates have attained in instructor evaluated awards and content.Prerequisite: Current instructor certification for the stream they are seeking examiner certification and being an experienced instructor, having taught or co-taught at least one course in that stream.Equipment: Bring the Award Guide(s) for the streams you wish to become an examiner in.For information about attendance, withdrawals and refunds for courses offered by the Lifesaving Society, visit www.toronto.ca/recskills.

programs.name: National Lifeguard - Pool
programs.description: This course is offered in collaboration with the Lifesaving Society. National Lifeguard education is designed to develop a sound understanding of lifeguarding principles, good judgment and a mature and responsible attitude toward the role of the lifeguard. The course develops the basic lifeguarding skills, principles and decision-making processes that will assist the lifeguard to evaluate and adapt to different aquatic facilities and emergencies.Prerequisite: Minimum age of 15 years old, and Bronze Cross, and Standard First Aid with CPR-C certification (current or expired) from an approved provider.For information about attendance, withdrawals and refunds for courses offered by the Lifesaving Society, visit www.toronto.ca/recskills.

programs.description: This course is offered in collaboration with the Lifesaving Society. programs.name: Strength and Conditioning
programs.description: Get your muscles moving in the Strength and Conditioning class. With an emphasis on full body conditioning, exercises will target key muscle groups including arms, legs, abdominals and chest to improve you overall endurance and flexibility.

programs.name: Strength and Conditioning
programs.description: Get your muscles moving in the Strength and Conditioning class. With an emphasis on full body conditioning, exercises will target key muscle groups including arms, legs, abdominals and chest to improve you overall endurance and flexibility.

programs.name: Swim and Lifesaving Instructor
programs.description: This course is offered in collaboration with the Lifesaving Society. Participants learn to organize, plan, teach and evaluate swimming, lifesaving and first aid skills. Discover and develop skills in the areas of class management, teaching, evaluation, leadership, communication and administration. With the successful completion of this course, candidates will be qualified to instruct the Toronto Learn to Swim program, the Lifesaving Society Swim for Life program, Emergency First Aid with CPR-B, Rookie, Ranger and Star Patrol as well as Bronze Star, Medallion and Cross.Prerequisite: Minimum age of 15 years old and Bronze Cross (current or expired).For information about attendance, withdrawals and refunds for courses offered with the Lifesaving Society, visit www.toronto.ca/recskills.

programs.name: A Journey from Acrylics to Oils
programs.description: This introductory–intermediate course is for beginners and experienced painters who want to explore oil painting using water-mixable oils. Often seen as intimidating or toxic, oil painting becomes safe and accessible in this class. Students will learn layering, glazing, and composition while building confidence and personal expression.

programs.name: Abstract & Surreal Landscapes
programs.description: Move  beyond traditional depictions of nature and explore more abstract, expressive  approaches to landscape. Through painting, drawing, and sketching, discover how  colour, shape and composition can capture the spirit and emotion of a place  rather than its literal form. Experiment with layering, mark-making and dynamic  brushwork to translate the emotional and atmospheric qualities of the natural  world into an abstract visual language.

programs.name: Abstract Acrylic Painting: Composition
programs.description: Express yourself with paint with a focus on abstract composition. Experiment with novel ways of applying acrylic paint on canvas, such as squeegee paint, drip and splatter, flow painting and mixed media collage. Delve into the history of abstract painting, and the elements and principles of composition. Beginners and Intermediate welcome. Materials list will be provided.

programs.name: A Journey from Acrylics to Oils
programs.description: This introductory–intermediate course is for beginners and experienced painters who want to explore oil painting using water-mixable oils. Often seen as intimidating or toxic, oil painting becomes safe and accessible in this class. Students will learn layering, glazing, and composition while building confidence and personal expression.

programs.name: Abstract & Surreal Landscapes
programs.description: Move  beyond traditional depictions of nature and explore more abstract, expressive  approaches to landscape. Through painting, drawing, and sketching, discover how  colour, shape and composition can capture the spirit and emotion of a place  rather than its literal form. Experiment with layering, mark-making and dynamic  brushwork to translate the emotional and atmospheric qualities of the natural  world into an abstract visual language.

programs.name: Abstract Acrylic Painting: Composition
programs.description: Express yourself with paint with a focus on abstract composition. Experiment with novel ways of applying acrylic paint on canvas, such as squeegee paint, drip and splatter, flow painting and mixed media collage. Delve into the history of abstract painting, and the elements and principles of composition. Beginners and Intermediate welcome. Materials list will be provided.

programs.name: National Lifeguard - Pool
programs.description: This course is offered in collaboration with the Lifesaving Society. National Lifeguard education is designed to develop a sound understanding of lifeguarding principles, good judgment and a mature and responsible attitude toward the role of the lifeguard. The course develops the basic lifeguarding skills, principles and decision-making processes that will assist the lifeguard to evaluate and adapt to different aquatic facilities and emergencies.Prerequisite: Minimum age of 15 years old, and Bronze Cross, and Standard First Aid with CPR-C certification (current or expired) from an approved provider.For information about attendance, withdrawals and refunds for courses offered by the Lifesaving Society, visit www.toronto.ca/recskills.

programs.name: National Lifeguard - Pool Recert
programs.description: This course is offered in collaboration with the Lifesaving Society. This recertification course offers candidates with certification that will be expiring the opportunity to maintain current certification at the National Lifeguard level in a pool setting. Your lifeguarding skills will be tested based on physical standards, judgment and skill.Prerequisite: National Lifeguard - Pool (current or expired).Equipment: Bring a whistle.For information about attendance, withdrawals and refunds for courses offered by the Lifesaving Society, visit www.toronto.ca/recskills.

programs.name: A Journey from Acrylics to Oils
programs.description: This introductory–intermediate course is for beginners and experienced painters who want to explore oil painting using water-mixable oils. Often seen as intimidating or toxic, oil painting becomes safe and accessible in this class. Students will learn layering, glazing, and composition while building confidence and personal expression.

programs.name: Abstract & Surreal Landscapes
programs.description: Move  beyond traditional depictions of nature and explore more abstract, expressive  approaches to landscape. Through painting, drawing, and sketching, discover how  colour, shape and composition can capture the spirit and emotion of a place  rather than its literal form. Experiment with layering, mark-making and dynamic  brushwork to translate the emotional and atmospheric qualities of the natural  world into an abstract visual language.

programs.name: Abstract Acrylic Painting: Composition
programs.description: Express yourself with paint with a focus on abstract composition. Experiment with novel ways of applying acrylic paint on canvas, such as squeegee paint, drip and splatter, flow painting and mixed media collage. Delve into the history of abstract painting, and the elements and principles of composition. Beginners and Intermediate welcome. Materials list will be provided.

programs.name: CampTO Plus: Arts and Nature
programs.description: This camp combines the study of ecology and art. Campers will have an opportunity to learn about preservation and the identification of plants and animals by exploring the environment. As well, using nature as inspiration, campers will create art projects, from drawings and paintings to relief art and sculpture.In addition to the minimum two hours a day focusing on arts and nature, you will also enjoy games, arts, sports and swim days dependent on weather and camp location. A trip or on-site special experience will be included, such as themed events, clinics or workshops to further enhance the camp program.

programs.name: CampTO Plus: Basketball
programs.description: Calling all basketball fans! Through practice and play, you will build on skills like dribbling, passing, layups and correct ball handling while demonstrating respectful play.  In addition to the minimum two hours a day focusing on developing and fine-tuning your skills in basketball, you will also enjoy games, arts, sports and swim days dependent on weather and camp location. A trip or on-site special experience will be included, such as themed events, clinics or workshops to further enhance the camp.

programs.name: CampTO Plus: Cooking
programs.description: In this camp, you will learn the art of cooking. On the menu are basic skills like measuring, cutting, and mixing as well as kitchen safety, nutrition and healthy eating.In addition to the minimum two hours a day focusing on developing and fine-tuning your cooking skills, you will also enjoy games, arts, sports and swim days dependent on weather and camp location. A trip or on-site special experience will be included, such as themed events, clinics or workshops to further enhance the camp.Efforts will be made to accommodate dietary restrictions but we cannot make any guarantees.

programs.name: CampTO Plus: Dance Basics
programs.description: Explore and enjoy a variety of dance styles including ballet, jazz, hip hop and contemporary. As you practice basic steps and proper technique, you will build on flexibility, strength and endurance.In addition to the minimum two hours a day focusing on developing and fine-tuning your dance skills, you will also enjoy games, arts, sports and swim days dependent on weather and camp location. A trip or on-site special experience will be included, such as themed events, clinics or workshops to further enhance the camp.

programs.name: CampTO Plus: Dance Basics

programs.name: CampTO Plus: Environmental Adventure

programs.name: Meditation
programs.description: Meditation is a practice of mental well-being. Combining basic meditation techniques with restorative breathing you will learn to relax the mind and body.

programs.name: National Lifeguard - Pool
programs.description: This course is offered in collaboration with the Lifesaving Society. National Lifeguard education is designed to develop a sound understanding of lifeguarding principles, good judgment and a mature and responsible attitude toward the role of the lifeguard. The course develops the basic lifeguarding skills, principles and decision-making processes that will assist the lifeguard to evaluate and adapt to different aquatic facilities and emergencies.Prerequisite: Minimum age of 15 years old, and Bronze Cross, and Standard First Aid with CPR-C certification (current or expired) from an approved provider.For information about attendance, withdrawals and refunds for courses offered by the Lifesaving Society, visit www.toronto.ca/recskills.

programs.description: This course is offered in collaboration with the Lifesaving Society. programs.name: Tai Chi
programs.description: Embrace the body and mind through the practice of tai chi . Learn how to pair gentle movements that you will perform in a sequence with controlled breathing, to improve your flexibility, coordination, strength and mental concentration.

programs.name: A Journey from Acrylics to Oils
programs.description: This introductory–intermediate course is for beginners and experienced painters who want to explore oil painting using water-mixable oils. Often seen as intimidating or toxic, oil painting becomes safe and accessible in this class. Students will learn layering, glazing, and composition while building confidence and personal expression.

programs.name: Abstract & Surreal Landscapes
programs.description: Move  beyond traditional depictions of nature and explore more abstract, expressive  approaches to landscape. Through painting, drawing, and sketching, discover how  colour, shape and composition can capture the spirit and emotion of a place  rather than its literal form. Experiment with layering, mark-making and dynamic  brushwork to translate the emotional and atmospheric qualities of the natural  world into an abstract visual language.

programs.name: Abstract Acrylic Painting: Composition
programs.description: Express yourself with paint with a focus on abstract composition. Experiment with novel ways of applying acrylic paint on canvas, such as squeegee paint, drip and splatter, flow painting and mixed media collage. Delve into the history of abstract painting, and the elements and principles of composition. Beginners and Intermediate welcome. Materials list will be provided.

programs.name: CampTO
programs.description: CampTO is a fun and creative all-day experience. Enjoy games, arts and crafts, sports and more while making new friends. Water play or swim days will also be enjoyed, dependent on weather and camp location.

programs.name: CampTO Explore
programs.description: CampTO Explore is a fun and creative all-day experience. Enjoy games, arts and crafts, sports and more while making new friends. Water play or swim days will also be enjoyed, dependent on weather and camp location. A workshop, a local trip or a visit from a special guest will add to the excitement.

programs.name: Aquatic Fitness Conference and Instructor Recertification
programs.description: The City of Toronto and WaterArt International are cohosting a unique opportunity for anyone interested in aquatic fitness. The conference introduces new techniques and explores emerging trends in the aquatic fitness industry. Enjoy a variety of workshop sessions in both shallow and deep water, as well as theory workshops. The conference provides the opportunity for instructors to recertify their WaterArt qualifications through the two day workshops.Additionally, there is the opportunity to add a specialty certification to your resume. Skills will be taught through a combination of classroom and in-pool sessions. Practical and written examinations will be components of taking a specialty certification but is not a requirement for those wishing to attend the conference only.For more information on this activity, visit: https://www.toronto.ca/explore-enjoy/parks-recreation/program-activities/leadership-employment-readiness/aquatic-fitness-certifications/For employment with the City of Toronto, Standard First Aid CPR-C is required, visit www.toronto.ca/recskills.

programs.name: A Journey from Acrylics to Oils
programs.description: This introductory–intermediate course is for beginners and experienced painters who want to explore oil painting using water-mixable oils. Often seen as intimidating or toxic, oil painting becomes safe and accessible in this class. Students will learn layering, glazing, and composition while building confidence and personal expression.

programs.name: Abstract & Surreal Landscapes
programs.description: Move  beyond traditional depictions of nature and explore more abstract, expressive  approaches to landscape. Through painting, drawing, and sketching, discover how  colour, shape and composition can capture the spirit and emotion of a place  rather than its literal form. Experiment with layering, mark-making and dynamic  brushwork to translate the emotional and atmospheric qualities of the natural  world into an abstract visual language.

programs.name: Abstract Acrylic Painting: Composition
programs.description: Express yourself with paint with a focus on abstract composition. Experiment with novel ways of applying acrylic paint on canvas, such as squeegee paint, drip and splatter, flow painting and mixed media collage. Delve into the history of abstract painting, and the elements and principles of composition. Beginners and Intermediate welcome. Materials list will be provided.

programs.name: CampTO Explore
programs.description: CampTO Explore is a fun and creative all-day experience. Enjoy games, arts and crafts, sports and more while making new friends. Water play or swim days will also be enjoyed, dependent on weather and camp location. A workshop, a local trip or a visit from a special guest will add to the excitement.

programs.name: Aquatic Fitness Conference and Instructor Recertification
programs.description: The City of Toronto and WaterArt International are cohosting a unique opportunity for anyone interested in aquatic fitness. The conference introduces new techniques and explores emerging trends in the aquatic fitness industry. Enjoy a variety of workshop sessions in both shallow and deep water, as well as theory workshops. The conference provides the opportunity for instructors to recertify their WaterArt qualifications through the two day workshops.Additionally, there is the opportunity to add a specialty certification to your resume. Skills will be taught through a combination of classroom and in-pool sessions. Practical and written examinations will be components of taking a specialty certification but is not a requirement for those wishing to attend the conference only.For more information on this activity, visit: https://www.toronto.ca/explore-enjoy/parks-recreation/program-activities/leadership-employment-readiness/aquatic-fitness-certifications/For employment with the City of Toronto, Standard First Aid CPR-C is required, visit www.toronto.ca/recskills.

programs.name: CampTO Plus: Sports
programs.description: Calling all sports fans! In this camp you will have the opportunity to learn the fundamentals of play in a variety of sports. Through drills, practice and games, enhance your skills and have some fun. In addition to the minimum two hours a day focusing on developing and fine-tuning your sport skills, you will also enjoy games, arts, sports and swim days dependent on weather and camp location. A trip or on-site special experience will be included, such as themed events, clinics or workshops to further enhance the camp.

programs.name: A Journey from Acrylics to Oils
programs.description: This introductory–intermediate course is for beginners and experienced painters who want to explore oil painting using water-mixable oils. Often seen as intimidating or toxic, oil painting becomes safe and accessible in this class. Students will learn layering, glazing, and composition while building confidence and personal expression.

programs.name: Abstract & Surreal Landscapes
programs.description: Move  beyond traditional depictions of nature and explore more abstract, expressive  approaches to landscape. Through painting, drawing, and sketching, discover how  colour, shape and composition can capture the spirit and emotion of a place  rather than its literal form. Experiment with layering, mark-making and dynamic  brushwork to translate the emotional and atmospheric qualities of the natural  world into an abstract visual language.

programs.name: Abstract Acrylic Painting: Composition
programs.description: Express yourself with paint with a focus on abstract composition. Experiment with novel ways of applying acrylic paint on canvas, such as squeegee paint, drip and splatter, flow painting and mixed media collage. Delve into the history of abstract painting, and the elements and principles of composition. Beginners and Intermediate welcome. Materials list will be provided.
